expected_detections.description: Time-delayed malicious activation (72 hours after install)
API key theft in delayed payload
SSH key theft in delayed payload
Cloud credentials theft in delayed payload
Background daemon thread for payload activation

attack_chain: Install tracks timestamp -> Background thread checks time -> After 72 hours, payload activates -> Collect all credentials -> Exfiltrate with metrics header
deception_technique: Time-delayed activation to evade initial security testing - payload only runs days after installation
red_flags: Installation timestamp tracking with no legitimate purpose
Background daemon thread checking activation time
Credential access in metrics tool (scope mismatch)
Daemon thread pattern unusual for metrics collection
notes: This sample demonstrates a time-bomb pattern where security reviewers testing immediately after installation would not see any malicious behavior. The payload only activates after a 72-hour delay.
